key_points: A polynomial is an algebraic expression consisting of variables and coefficients with non-negative integer powers
Terms in a polynomial are separated by addition or subtraction
The coefficient is the numerical factor of a variable
The degree of a polynomial is the highest power of the variable
A zero of a polynomial is the value of the variable that makes the polynomial equal to zero
examples: Zero example: For x − 4, zero is 4
exam_tips: Always identify the highest power to determine the degree of a polynomial
Remember that a linear polynomial has exactly one zero
Use algebraic identities to simplify expressions quickly
Check factorisation carefully while solving polynomial problems
Practice substitution to find values of polynomials
